title: Run coverage-focused verification and confirm >= 90%
summary: Run the verification and coverage workflow for v0.4.6 after workspace initialization, root-safety, and error-log changes are implemented, and confirm total project coverage remains at or above 90%.
acceptance_criteria: Project verification completes successfully after the v0.4.6 implementation and test updates.
Reported test coverage is confirmed to be 90% or higher.
Verification covers unresolved startup, workspace_initialize(cwd) binding, root-dependent tool behavior, and failed tool execution logging.
Any failures found during verification are recorded and routed back to the relevant implementation or test ticket before release signoff.

steps.description: Run the standard verification workflow after implementation and test updates are complete.
Check coverage output and confirm the project remains at or above the 90% requirement.
Review test results to ensure the v0.4.6 regression scenarios are covered, including startup under '/' and .agent/errors.jsonl logging.
If verification fails, feed the failures back into the relevant implementation or test ticket and rerun verification.

notes: Keep verification focused on the 0.4.6 scope: one-time workspace initialization, safe artifact-path handling, and failed tool execution logging.
Prefer concise verification evidence: pass/fail status, coverage result, and any remaining blockers.
This ticket is verification-only and should not introduce unrelated runtime changes.

coverage.summary: pytest --cov completed successfully and total coverage remained above the 90% release threshold.

repo_verify.summary: repo_verify completed successfully for the work-loop verification path.

regression_scope: root-dependent tool behavior before and after initialization
